commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=2358aad9b1906f77c34306fc117d4b82588d50b4)
commands.review_summary:
Replaces GitHub-based skill backups with R2/S3-compatible registry artifact backup and restore flows, adds package artifact coverage, durable retry state, seed/backfill tooling, and a manual migration backup gate.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: a user without a handle can publish under a display-name-derived root while seed/retry uses the user ID, producing two deterministic object paths for the same skill. No live bucket reproduction was run in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow identity fix is clear, but collaborator-authored production migration, credential cutover, historical seed, and contributor-owned real behavior proof require maintainer handling before merge.

target.title: Back up registry artifacts and add migration backup gate
target.body:
## Summary

- Replace the stale GitHub artifact backup path with R2/S3-compatible registry artifact backups for hosted skill versions and plugin/package release artifacts.
- Store a browsable restore catalog under `skills/` and `packages/` with per-version `_meta.json` files plus root `_index.json` files.
- Keep publish-time backup async and non-fatal, queue failed writes as retry jobs, and keep the recurring cron retry-only so it does not scan the full registry.
- Add explicit admin seed/backfill support via `registryArtifactBackups:seedRegistryArtifactBackups` for the initial historical population.
- Add the migration lifecycle skill/manual Convex backup gate without automating dashboard backup creation.

## Operator Notes

Required Convex env:

- `REGISTRY_BACKUP_R2_ACCOUNT_ID` or `REGISTRY_BACKUP_S3_ENDPOINT`
- `REGISTRY_BACKUP_BUCKET`
- `REGISTRY_BACKUP_ACCESS_KEY_ID`
- `REGISTRY_BACKUP_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY`

Optional:

- `REGISTRY_BACKUP_S3_REGION`
- `REGISTRY_BACKUP_SKILLS_ROOT`
- `REGISTRY_BACKUP_PACKAGES_ROOT`

Smoke after deploy:

- Run a dry-run seed with a tiny batch.
- Run a real seed with `batchSize: 1, maxBatches: 1`.
- Inspect the R2 `_meta.json` and `_index.json` objects for one backed-up skill/package.
- Start the controlled seed/backfill separately.

## Proof

```bash
CONVEX_DEPLOYMENT=dev:small-squirrel-75 VITE_CONVEX_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3212 bunx convex codegen --typecheck=disable
# passed

bun run test convex/lib/registryArtifactBackup.test.ts convex/registryArtifactBackups.test.ts convex/registryArtifactRestore.test.ts convex/functions.test.ts convex/crons.test.ts
# 5 files passed, 41 tests passed

bun run format:check -- CONTRIBUTING.md specs/spec.md specs/deploy.md convex/lib/registryArtifactBackup.ts convex/lib/registryArtifactBackup.test.ts convex/registryArtifactBackups.ts convex/registryArtifactBackups.test.ts convex/registryArtifactBackupsNode.ts convex/registryArtifactRestore.ts convex/registryArtifactRestore.test.ts convex/registryArtifactRestoreMutations.ts convex/httpApiV1/usersV1.ts convex/crons.ts convex/crons.test.ts convex/functions.ts convex/functions.test.ts convex/lib/skillPublish.ts convex/packages.ts convex/schema.ts convex/skills.ts convex/_generated/api.d.ts
# passed

bunx oxlint --type-aware --tsconfig ./tsconfig.oxlint.json convex/lib/registryArtifactBackup.ts convex/registryArtifactBackups.ts convex/registryArtifactBackupsNode.ts convex/registryArtifactRestore.ts convex/registryArtifactRestoreMutations.ts convex/crons.ts convex/functions.ts convex/httpApiV1/usersV1.ts convex/skills.ts
# passed

bunx tsc -p tsconfig.json --noEmit --pretty false
# passed

bun run --cwd packages/schema build
# passed

CONVEX_DEPLOYMENT=dev:small-squirrel-75 VITE_CONVEX_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3212 bunx convex dev --once --typecheck=disable
# passed; Convex functions ready

git diff --check
# passed
```

## Review

- `codex review --uncommitted` exited cleanly with no accepted/actionable findings reported after the restore-safety fixes.
